designs.ffi_constraints: PackageManager is NOT held via ParentRef/JsCell from C++ — no .classes.ts entry, no src/jsc/bindings/ references. It IS a leaked process-singleton stored in `static holder::RAW_PTR: AtomicPtr<PackageManager>` (PackageManager.rs:739) and accessed from thread-pool workers via `PackageManager::get() -> &'static PackageManager`. Two callback paths pass `*mut PackageManager` erased to `*mut c_void`: (1) `on_wake.get_handler()(ctx, this as *mut c_void)` at PackageManager.rs:911-916 (resolver-hook / auto_installer.rs:310), and (2) `event_loop.tick_once/tick_raw(ctx)` at PackageManagerLifecycle.rs:234-258. Constraint: the OUTER struct's address must remain stable and the type must stay a single struct at that address — so field-grouping into a nested `sched: TaskScheduler` is fine (outer address unchanged, callbacks still receive the same `*mut PackageManager`), but splitting into two separate top-level singletons would break the wake-handler/event-loop ctx round-trip. No callback frees the struct (it is leaked for process lifetime), so the `borrow = ptr` provenance mode is not required on ordinary methods.

run_resolver:2115, process_resolve_queue:6457, log_for_resolution_failures call site:2249 — read while &mut Transpiler from transpiler_for_target is held

resolver/enqueue paths that write import_records / append input_files while build_graphs map slot is held
log_for_resolution_failures(&mut self)->&mut Log at :2249 — held while self.graph.ast/input_files and self.transpiler.options are read
is_done:1999 — Graph::drain_deferred_tasks takes (&mut self.graph, &mut BundleV2) simultaneously

find_reachable_files :1892/:1901/:1963/:1968, scan_for_secondary_paths :2070, LinkerContext::load :560 — MultiArrayList column split_mut() alongside sibling-column shared reads (intra-Graph SoA, not cross-BundleV2-field)

designs.ffi_constraints:
SEVERE — BundleV2 is held by raw pointer from many places and has three container_of-recovered fields:
(1) container_of: `from_field_ptr!(BundleV2, linker, ..)` at LinkerContext.rs:251, `from_field_ptr!(BundleV2, graph, ..)` at HTMLImportManifest.rs:182, `from_field_ptr!(BundleV2, drain_defer_task, ..)` at DeferredBatchTask.rs:36. These three fields MUST remain direct fields of BundleV2 at stable offsets — cannot be moved into a sub-struct.
(2) Raw-ptr holders: `ParseTask.ctx: Option<ParentRef<BundleV2<'static>>>`, `ServerComponentParseTask.ctx`, `Worker.ctx: BackRef<BundleV2<'static>>` (thread-pool workers), `Resolve.bv2`/`Load.bv2: *mut BundleV2<'static>` (JS-thread plugin dispatch), `NewHotReloader<BundleV2,..>` (watcher thread under --watch holds `NonNull<BundleV2>`). All use lifetime-erased `'static` casts.
(3) C++ side: no direct `JsCell`/codegen wrapper for BundleV2, but `JSBundlerPlugin__matchOnLoad/matchOnResolve` receive `*mut c_void` whose JS completion callback mutates/consumes the Resolve/Load which embeds `*mut BundleV2` — so those two sites (:1132/:1265) are genuine reentrant-callback provenance, not borrowck workarounds.
(4) `LinkerContext::link`/`load` take `bundle: *mut BundleV2` and reborrow — splitting BundleV2 into separate allocations would break the `&mut this.linker` + `*mut BundleV2` pattern at bundle_v2.rs:3815/3980/4908/5291.
Net: field-grouping is ONLY safe for fields NOT in {graph, linker, drain_defer_task}. The transpiler cluster qualifies; nothing else meaningfully does.

jsc_hooks.rs:903 auto_tick, jsc_hooks.rs:1066 auto_tick_active — hold &mut EventLoop while reading/swapping sibling VM fields; tick_immediate_tasks(vm: *mut VirtualMachine) re-enters arbitrary JS so any split is defeated.
jsc_hooks.rs:670 load_preloads — iterate vm.preload while resolver + event_loop.wait_for_promise need &mut. wait_for_promise re-enters JS.
VirtualMachine.rs:3781 ref_counted_string_with_was_new — captures NonNull<self> as RefString.ctx before ref_strings.entry(); FFI-provenance constraint (ctx later used by C finalizer to mutate VM).

VirtualMachine.rs:4313 — RestoreLog drop-guard stores BackRef<VM> and writes log/transpiler.resolver.log on drop; body re-derives &mut from raw jsc_vm_ptr. difficulty.json flags this as redundant (NLL already ends the prior borrow).

VirtualMachine.rs:5527 (zig_exception::Holder), hot_reloader.rs:863 (Watcher), jsc_hooks.rs:2372 (stack locals in scopeguard), jsc_hooks.rs:4203 (Option<Blob> move) — 4 of the 15 sites are unrelated to the VirtualMachine struct.

designs.ffi_constraints: C++ `ZigGlobalObject` stores the whole struct as `void* m_bunVM` (set from `Bun__getVM()`), and ~38 `HOST_EXPORT`/`#[no_mangle]` thunks take `*mut VirtualMachine` / `&VirtualMachine` directly — the allocation cannot be split into separate heap objects and field offsets of anything read from C++ must stay reachable from the one opaque pointer. `event_loop: *mut EventLoop` is a self-pointer into the sibling `regular_event_loop`/`macro_event_loop` value fields, so those two cannot move out of the struct. Site 3781 stores `NonNull<VirtualMachine>` in `RefString.ctx` for a WTF external-string C finalizer — that pointer must derive from a `*mut` (SharedReadWrite provenance), so a `&self`-derived capture would be UB (`keep` per difficulty.json). `src/CLAUDE.md` provenance rule: callbacks that may free/mutate self must dispatch off `*mut Self`; auto_tick/auto_tick_active/load_preloads fall under this (they re-enter JS which calls `VirtualMachine::get_mut()`), so their raw-ptr pattern is correct, not a borrowck workaround. VirtualMachine is not held via a generated `.classes.ts` JsCell/ParentRef — it is the per-thread singleton (`#[thread_local] static VM: Cell<Option<*mut VirtualMachine>>`) with `as_mut(&self) -> &mut Self` as the documented JsCell-style escape hatch.

conflict_groups.used_by: module_info() accessor at lib.rs:1468 — sites 1833, 4752, 4908, 5488, 6007 all write add_var/add_export_info/request_module

name_for_symbol() (reads renamer) and str.slice(self.bump) — needed WHILE holding &mut module_info at the 5 sites above; fields are disjoint from module_info so the conflict is purely accessor-induced
symbols().get_const()/get_with_link_const() returning &Symbol — sites 3942, 5288, 5359 hold the &Symbol across subsequent print_* calls
options.commonjs_named_exports (Option<&'a CommonJSNamedExports>, Copy) — site 3069 holds key slice across print_* calls
every print_*() method (~162 &mut self methods) — conflicts with any live &self-derived borrow at sites 3069, 3942, 5288, 5359

designs.ffi_constraints: None blocking. `Printer` is a generic stack-local constructed inside free fns `print_ast`/`print_with_writer`/`print_json` (lib.rs:6696 init, lib.rs:7805 print) and dropped before return. It is never held by C++: no `#[no_mangle]`/`extern` on it, no `.classes.ts` binding, and the only JsCell in this subsystem wraps `BufferWriter` (the `W` type param) at src/runtime/api/JSTranspiler.rs:48, not `Printer`. The `ParentRef` hits in renamer.rs:642/752 are on `NumberScope` (an internal tree node), not on `Printer` or `Renamer`. No callback can free `Printer` mid-print. Therefore layout/field-split changes are unconstrained by FFI; the only provenance concern is the existing `'a` launder in `name_for_symbol`, which the design preserves unchanged.

conflict_groups.used_by: handle_data_frame / handle_rst_stream_frame / handle_priority_frame / handle_continuation_frame / handle_headers_frame — each derefs a *mut Stream (heap-boxed value stored in self.streams) to &mut Stream and holds it across the handler body

same 5 handlers, via self.send_go_away / self.dispatch_with_* / self.adjust_window_size / self.handle_incomming_payload / self.write while the &mut Stream above is live — these fire JS callbacks that can RE-ENTER the parser and mutate self.streams
increment_window_size_if_needed (line 2288) — iterates self.streams.get().iter()
increment_window_size_if_needed → send_window_update → self.write, which can re-enter JS and mutate self.streams, so the iterator cannot be held across the call — hence the collect-to-Vec

designs.ffi_constraints: HARD BLOCKERS on splitting the struct: (1) `#[bun_jsc::JsClass]` codegen (src/runtime/api/h2.classes.ts → generated JSH2FrameParser C++ wrapper) holds `*mut H2FrameParser` and walks named JsRef/Strong fields for GC tracing — layout is pinned. (2) `#[derive(bun_ptr::RefCounted)]` with intrusive `ref_count: RefCount<Self>` — refcount lives inside the struct. (3) `IntrusiveRc<H2FrameParser>` stored in `SocketBody::H2` (src/runtime/socket/socket_body.rs:3950). (4) `as_ctx_ptr()` hands `*mut Self` to uSockets AutoFlusher; callback may free self (deinit via finalize). Per src/CLAUDE.md provenance rules, callbacks that may free self require `*mut Self` dispatch; the fully-Cell/&self model (R-2) is the approved answer and is already in place. The struct CANNOT be split into sub-structs without rewriting the JsClass codegen, the socket attachment, and the refcount plumbing.
